title: #finance: 2387654329
preview:
Sofia Martinez: FYI - Ora Systems returned SOW asking for 55% off for first 3 months + Net60 billing and waived onboarding fees. Can we approve?

Ethan Park: Account owned by Ethan (AM). Pilot 3mo, est 40M tokens/month (mix gen + embeddings). Potential Dedicated in H1 next year.

Priya Singh: Finance flag: 55% is outside standard pilot concessions. We usually cap at 35% for new logos unless there is a clear ARR commit. Net60 on a new account increases DSO. Any upstream PO or finance guarantee?

Sofia Martinez: Customer says legal requires Net60. They can issue PO but not a prepay. They're sensitive to onboarding cost; asked for waived $6k setup fee.

Markus Voss: From Revenue Ops perspective, we prefer to keep list price on invoices and apply a contra credit for any one-off onboarding waiver. That preserves revenue recognition. We also need credit checks if Net60.

Priya Singh: Proposal from finance ops for deal desk:
```
Item: Pilot access (40M tokens/mo) - list $4,500/mo
Requested: 55% off months 1-3 + waive $6k onboarding fee + Net60
Finance counter: 35% off months 1-3, $3k onboarding credit (one-time contra), Net30 billing. Require PO + finance-approved credit holdback for Net60.
```

Ethan Park: Sales can push for PO and accept 35% + $3k credit if that keeps the deal moving. Customer will try to get legal to accept Net30 but may not.

Sofia Martinez: If they insist on Net60 we could accept with an approved credit instrument or SCC (sales credit condemnation) per policy, but that needs VP-Finance sign-off.

approvals-bot: Approval ticket created: APP-4912. Assigned to Priya Singh and VP-Finance (Markus Voss cc). Summary: Ora Systems pilot pricing exception (requested 55% + waive setup + Net60). Link: https://internal/approvals/APP-4912 :white_check_mark:

Markus Voss: I'll review risk & collections exposure. If credit check passes and legal provides PO, I can approve conditional Net60 with a 90-day review and a $25k credit cap. Otherwise we require Net30.

Ethan Park: Great — I'll get the PO and confirm with customer. Holding on signature until APP-4912 resolved. Thanks.

title: #finance: 1907654321
preview:
Ethan Park: Hey @finance — weird one from last night. Took an approved rideshare (Uber) to the airport but the driver canceled and I had to hop in a taxi. I used my personal card for the taxi — taxi gave a non-itemized paper receipt for the fare (€48) and I also tipped the driver cash (€8). The Uber charge later showed up pending on the corp card for €52 (canceled-trip fee) even though I never accepted the Uber. How should I file this? Can I get the taxi + tip reimbursed and the corp card canceled-trip fee refunded?
Maria Gomez: Sorry that happened. Two parts: (1) Re taxi + tip: corporate policy reimburses necessary ground transport when corp card cannot be used or driver unavailable. We need the taxi receipt + a one-line note explaining the corp card attempt. Tips in cash are reimbursable up to company cap (€10 per ride) when incidental to travel; please note who you tipped and why. (2) Canceled-trip fee on corp card: Finance will not auto-approve charges on the corp card for trips you didn't take. We'll open a card dispute with the payments team once you submit the expense and provide the Uber cancellation email or screenshot.
Ethan Park: I have the taxi receipt photo and a bank screenshot of the personal card charge. Uber sent a cancellation email that shows fee applied (I can forward). Should I file two separate expenses (one for taxi+tip, one to dispute the corp charge) or submit as a single thread?
Lena Torres: Travel Ops: Submit one expense for the taxi + tip (tag as travel - ground). Attach: taxi receipt photo, bank transaction screenshot, and a brief note: "Corp ride-share canceled; personal taxi used; corp card charged cancellation fee." For the corp charge dispute, forward the Uber email to payments@redwood and attach the payments case number to the expense. We prefer one expense entry for your out-of-pocket amount and a separate ticket for the corp card dispute so tracking is clear.
Sam J: Quick policy note: cash tips reimbursable up to €10; if over you'll need manager approval. Also include the timestamp and the last 4 of the corp card attempted so payments can trace the cancellation fee.
Ethan Park: Uploads: taxi_receipt_190765.jpg (photo), personal_card_statement.png, Uber_cancel_190765.eml (forwarded). Fare was €48, tip €8, total €56. Uber cancellation fee €52 posted to corp card ending 1234. Submitting expense now and creating payments ticket.
Maria Gomez: Received your submission. I'll review attachments. If payments confirms the cancellation fee is erroneous we'll issue a reversal on the corp card and won't reimburse that charge. For your OOP taxi+tip: looks good and within tip cap. Please ensure expense note includes the attempted corp card swipe time and merchant name (Uber).
Ethan Park: Added note: "Attempted corp card charge to Uber at 22:15 local; driver canceled; personal taxi used to comply with flight schedule." Submitted expense #TR-2026-772 and payments ticket PT-5591.
Finance Bot: Policy excerpt: "When corporate card transactions occur for canceled or unused services, Finance will initiate a dispute. Employees should not be reimbursed for corporate card charges unless the charge is valid and documented. Cash tips are reimbursable up to €10 per ride when part of necessary travel. Required docs for OOP taxi reimbursements: receipt, personal card statement, explanation of corp card failure/attempt, manager approval if tip > cap." Link: https://intranet.redwood/fin/ground-transport
Maria Gomez: I'll process the OOP reimbursement after verifying documents. Payments team: please confirm dispute status on PT-5591; ping me once resolved. Thanks for clear attachments @Ethan :thumbsup:
Ethan Park: Thanks all  appreciate the quick help.

title: #finance: 1876543210
preview:
Anna Liu: Hey finance team — BoxedCo's March invoice rendered incorrectly in tonight's batch. The subscription line is missing and there's a phantom promo credit of -$1,200. PDF preview came back blank. Can someone take a look?
Raj Patel: Can you paste the invoice id and whether this was just for BoxedCo or larger batch?
Anna Liu: invoice id INV-2026-00789, single-customer run inside the nightly batch (customer-specific billing). Here's what the generation output gave us: 
```
{"id":"INV-2026-00789","customer":"BoxedCo","lines":[{"desc":"promo credit","amount_cents":-120000},{"desc":"tax","amount_cents":24000}],"total_cents":-96000}
```
Raj Patel: ugh — so subscription line (expected ~1,200,000 cents) is absent. Any render errors in logs?
Billing Bot: billing-renderer-2: failed to render PDF for INV-2026-00789 at 2026-03-23T02:14:07Z — error=\"missing template data: subscription_lines\" job=br-20260323-77
Maria Gomez: Likely the promo-sync job wrote a negative line before subscription aggregation completed, renderer saw partial invoice object and proceeded. Could be race between promotion reconciliation and invoice assembly. Please don't reissue yet if the invoice was already emailed.
Anna Liu: Not emailed yet. Customer preview not sent. We can regenerate.
Ben Carter: Short-term fix: re-run invoice generation for that invoice after pausing promo-sync. Command: `billing-cli invoice regenerate --id INV-2026-00789 --force` (this will update ledger). Need explicit approval from finance lead to mutate ledger.
Anna Liu: Approve re-run, go ahead. :thumbsup:
Raj Patel: Running now — pausing promo-sync, issuing regenerate...
Raj Patel: Result: regenerate returned success. New generated payload: 
```
{"id":"INV-2026-00789","customer":"BoxedCo","lines":[{"desc":"subscription (Mar)","amount_cents":1200000},{"desc":"tax","amount_cents":240000}],"total_cents":1440000}
```
Raj Patel: PDF preview rendered OK and attached to thread (preview link: https://files.redwood/invoices/INV-2026-00789/preview.pdf). Promo credit is no longer present.
Maria Gomez: Thanks. Please file a follow-up ticket to root-cause: possible race between promo-sync and invoice assembly + renderer not validating required fields. Assign to eng-billing and set P1 for next sprint planning.
Ben Carter: Filed ENG-5342: "Invoice assembly race causes missing lines / invalid render" and added steps to reproduce + logs. Assigning to @eng-billing.
Anna Liu: Great. I'll notify support to hold outreach to BoxedCo until we've confirmed final invoice.
Raj Patel: Closing thread for now — will re-open if anything else surfaces.

title: #finance: 1868004321
preview:
Priya Rao: Quick check-in on the token-count incident remediation — want to confirm owners/timelines for remaining actions (delayed ingestion backfill, invoice corrections for affected customers, recurring alert tuning).

Lina Gomez: Ack. From the postmortem notes: root cause = retries + missing idempotency on meter-ingest. Fix was merged yesterday but we still need backfill + invoice adjustments.

Carlos Mendez: Confirmed. Deployed idempotency key change at 08:20 UTC. No new duplicates since then. Backfill plan: run a dedupe + reconcile job over the ingestion window (2025-09-10 00:00 to 2025-09-12 14:00).

Jon Patel: What's the customer impact window and ETA for invoice corrections? We need a comms plan for any invoice re-issuance.

Lina Gomez: Impact window matches Carlos' range. Estimated affected invoices ~120 customers (mostly mid-size). Lina -> I'll own invoice corrections and comms. ETA: working batch corrections, target EOD Fri (in 5 business days).

Carlos Mendez: I'll own backfill job. I'll run a dry-run today then full job tomorrow. ETA: dry-run by 18:00 today, full backfill complete in 72 hours if no issues. Backfill job snippet:
```
python tools/reconcile_tokens.py --start 2025-09-10 --end 2025-09-12 --mode dryrun --idempotency-key ok
```

Samir "Sam" Khan: I'll tune/raise the recurring alert to trigger earlier on ingestion lag and add a histogram for retry spikes. I'll also add a paged alert to eng-ml if lag > 10m for >3mins. ETA: 48h.

Ava Chen: Noted this is the 3rd ingestion-delayed incident in the last 6 months. Recommend adding an end-to-end integration test in CI that simulates partial downstream outage + retry storm. Also suggest a weekly metering audit job that finds anomalous token spikes.

Priya Rao: Actions recap:
- Carlos: backfill + reconciliation job (dry-run today, finish in ~72h) :calendar: 
- Lina: invoice corrections & customer comms (EOD Fri) :email:
- Sam: alert tuning + paged alert (48h) :rotating_light:
- Ava: propose CI test + audit job (I'll sync with you next week)

Please react with :thumbsup: if you're aligned or reply with blocking issues. Let's do a 15-min sync tomorrow 09:30 UTC to review dry-run results.

billing-bot: Automated update: queued 2 correction batches for customer invoices. Job IDs: bc-4491, bc-4492. Status: queued.

Lina Gomez: Thanks — will update this thread with a link to the correction runbook and the customer template. Filing invoice corrections as ticket FIN-3824.

Carlos Mendez: Dry-run finished — found 0 duplicates in sampling. Scheduling full run at 02:00 UTC. Will post run logs here. 

Priya Rao: Perfect. If anyone hits blockers, ping me. We'll review the final postmortem with recurrence mitigations after the systems are stable. :thumbsup:

title: #finance: 1834567890
preview:
Lina Gomez: flagging invoice INV-2026-917 for HelioGrid — customer says platform fee and "third-party KMS pass-through" charge appear twice. They also claim support should be "Standard" per signed amendment, but invoice shows Platinum. Can ops triage? :sun_with_face:

Ethan Park: drop subscription id and invoice lines? I'll pull billing catalog and ledger.

Lina Gomez: sub: HEL-PRV-44. invoice extract:
```
INV-2026-917
Customer: HelioGrid Inc
Subscription: HEL-PRV-44
Private Deployment (reserved GPUs) - $60,000/year
Platform Fee - $15,000/year
Third-party KMS pass-through - $2,400/year
Support - Platinum - $18,000/year
Total Due: $95,400
```
Quoted renewal (amendment signed 2026-03-02) shows platform fee $7,500 and Support Standard $9,000 and KMS pass-through should be $2,400 but only once. Signed amendment in CRM under HEL-991.

Billing Bot: invoice INV-2026-917 generated by billing-service at 2026-06-22T16:09:30Z.

Nina (Acct Ops): initial glance: two likely causes: (1) platform fee true-up from extra short-term reserved capacity that got rolled into renewal, (2) billing catalog mapping mis-tagged support tier on renewal, or (3) a duplicate pass-through line was picked up because a vendor fee invoice was posted twice. I'll check ledger entries.

Ethan Park: ledger tail:
```
2026-04-01 HEL-PRV-44 reserve_pool create rp-777 dur=30d
2026-04-15 HEL-PRV-44 reserve_pool create rp-783 dur=14d
2026-05-10 billing-run trueup platform_fee_delta=+7500
2026-05-10 vendor-post KMS-INV-839 vendor_fee=2400
2026-06-10 vendor-post KMS-INV-839 vendor_fee=2400
```

Ethan Park: looks like the vendor invoice KMS-INV-839 was posted twice in June which explains the double KMS pass-through, and the platform true-up of +7,500 matches the unexpected increase from the two short reserve pools.

Lina Gomez: sales had verbally agreed to waive charges for the trial pools during renewal negotiation. Sam, can you confirm? Also the signed amendment clearly says Standard support.

Sam (Sales): confirmed — I approved the trial pools to be waived (email 2026-04-20) and the amendment downgrades support to Standard. Sales notes linked in CRM. The duplicate vendor posting was internal; I didn't see that. I'll forward my waiver email to Acct Ops and attach to the invoice.

Nina (Acct Ops): recommended actions:
- create credit for platform true-up $7,500 (waived per Sales)
- remove duplicated vendor pass-through $2,400 (credit) and correct vendor posting
- change support from Platinum ($18k) to Standard ($9k) and issue a correction credit of $9k
- reissue corrected invoice showing line-by-line credits and link signed amendment + sales waiver
Any objections?

Lina Gomez: sounds good. Finance approval needed for combined credits = $18,900? That's above our $15k self-approval. Oliver out sick today, who can sign? :thinking_face:

Ethan Park: note — combined credit = 7,500 + 2,400 + 9,000 = 18,900. We need Finance approval >$15k. I'll ping Oliver and cc Revenue.

Billing Bot: ticket BILL-2026-201 created for invoice correction. Estimated work: 1 hour.

Ethan Park: Oliver (Finance) approved via Slack DM (see thread); he cleared contract remediation credits up to $25k. Proceeding. :white_check_mark:

Nina (Acct Ops): credits queued: CR-2026-517 (platform waive) $7,500, CR-2026-518 (duplicate vendor) $2,400, CR-2026-519 (support tier correction) $9,000. Reissued invoice INV-2026-917-R. Memo: "Apply credits per signed amendment and sales-approved trial waiver; correct duplicate vendor posting."

Billing Bot: credits applied and invoice INV-2026-917-R issued at 2026-06-23T10:12:00Z. Attachments: HEL-991_amendment.pdf, sales_waiver_2026-04-20.eml.

Lina Gomez: great — please confirm RevRec posting date and that customer has received the updated bill. I'll close when confirmed.

Ethan Park: RevRec cc'd and will post credit in Q2. I emailed customer with updated invoice and payment instructions. Closing thread.

title: #finance: 1774506789
preview:
Aisha Noor: FYI Solstice Labs GmbH sent invoice SL-3389 for SaaS support billed to Redwood US and included 19% German VAT but DID NOT list their Umsatzsteuer-ID on the PDF. They also attached a signed W-8BEN-E. Should we hold payment? :eyes:

Ben Carter: German VAT + W-8BEN-E on the same doc feels off. For B2B cross-border services to a US legal entity, vendor normally should not charge German VAT (export of services). Also invoice must show supplier VAT ID if they charge VAT.

Lucia Romano: Agreed. W-8BEN-E is for US withholding/tax status of foreign entities and has no bearing on EU VAT. If Solstice is charging VAT they need to include their VAT number (USt-IdNr) and the basis for charging. If the supply is to a US customer, they should either zero-rate or state export of services.

Aisha Noor: I asked them to reissue invoice without VAT or provide a legal basis for charging VAT and to include their USt-IdNr. Told them we may delay payment until corrected invoice or credit note is issued.

tax-bot: Quick checks: 1) Verify vendor USt-IdNr via German BZSt (or VIES) 2) If vendor insists on charging VAT despite billing US entity, request VAT registration docs and an explanation of place-of-supply treatment.

Ben Carter: Also confirm where Solstice's billing entity is registered (DE branch vs EU holding). If they claim US source or subcontractor billing from non-EU entity, that changes treatment. But they sent a German address.

Lucia Romano: If vendor refuses to remove VAT and we pay it, we will need a credit note to reclaim on our end or treat it as non-recoverable cost depending on jurisdiction. Procurement should avoid gross-ups; if vendor asks Redwood to 'gross up' to cover their VAT, escalate to finance lead. We don't gross-up standard supplier invoices.

Solstice Labs (via email): "We are registered in DE. We charged VAT because system defaulted for EU entities. We have attached VAT registration screenshot." (attachment not yet verified)

Aisha Noor: I told them: please reissue with USt-IdNr on invoice, or else remove VAT and issue a zero-rated export invoice. Also asked them to confirm bank account matches invoicing legal entity.

payment-bot: Reminder set: follow up on SL-3389 in 4 business days.

Ben Carter: If they produce USt-IdNr, run VIES check and keep evidence. If VIES invalid, tag tax for manual review before payment.

Lucia Romano: Note for records: keep email trail and any VAT screenshots. If they insist on W-8 as proof, tell them W-8 only affects US withholding — not EU VAT.

Aisha Noor: Thanks all — putting a hold and will update once vendor replies. :white_check_mark:

title: #finance: 1770178450
preview:
Emily Stone: We need to align today on audit trail requirements for *meter changes* before we flip any fixes into billing. What exactly do we need to retain (evidence), and who must approve? Trying to avoid "we changed the counter" with no paper trail 🙃

Laura Bennett: +1. I want this to look like an internal control: who approved, what changed, when effective, and how we validated. If we ever get audited or have a dispute, we should be able to reconstruct.

Talia Benson: I can capture + publish a single process doc, but need the requirements first. Is this basically: Linear ticket + PR + Confluence change record + signoffs?

Michael Grant: From Legal perspective, anything that touches *usage definition* (tokens, retries, caching attribution) needs a clear record + customer-facing consistency. If definition changes, we should also capture whether contracts/Order Forms need updated language.

Sofia Mendes: + for Commercial. Also need to retain what we told Sales/CS to say (FAQ copy) and when it went live. In disputes, customers ask “what did this mean on Feb 1 vs Feb 20?”

Logan Wright: Eng view: we already have PRs + deploy logs, but we don’t have a single "meter change record". Happy to add a template. Question: do you need *data* snapshots too (shadow-billing diffs), or are code references enough?

Emily Stone: We need more than code references. Specifically for RevRec: (1) definition, (2) effective date/time, (3) impact assessment, (4) validation, (5) approvals.

Nadia Rahman: +1. For validation, we should store the shadow billing compare output for at least the first 2–4 weeks and for month-end close. Otherwise we’ll just say “looked good” with no reproducible artifact.

Mei Lin: For evidence, I’d like: the recon queries we ran (SQL + result counts) and the drift metrics pre/post. Doesn’t need full data dump, but at least the aggregated outputs.

Aisha Bello: For monitoring, we can retain alert history + dashboard snapshots (Grafana/Console panels). That plus the job run logs will show ongoing compliance to SLOs.

Connor O'Brien: Release-wise: we can gate any meter-affecting deploy behind a checklist. If it’s helpful, I can make a "meter change" label and require: shadow mode on, signoffs attached, rollback plan.

Laura Bennett: Let’s be explicit on *approvals*:
- Any change that could change billed usage or credits application: Finance approval (Emily or me)
- Any change that changes customer-facing definition/interpretation: Legal approval (Michael or Sofia)
- Eng owner approval (Logan/Nadia) and release owner (Connor)
Also, for material changes, I want a heads-up to Jordan (CRO) so Sales isn’t surprised by questions.

Michael Grant: Agree, and I’d add: we need a "contract impact" check box. Most changes won’t require contract updates, but token definition / retries / caching are exactly the redline topics.

Sofia Mendes: And if we decide not to update standard language, we still record that decision ("no contract impact") with who assessed it.

Talia Benson: Ok. Drafting a template now. Proposed required fields:
1) Meter name(s) + version (e.g., hosted_api_tokens_v3)
2) What changed (1–3 bullets)
3) Why (bug/accuracy/experiment)
4) Effective timestamp (UTC) + backfill? (Y/N)
5) Impact assessment (expected delta + affected cohorts/accounts)
6) Validation artifacts (shadow billing report link + recon queries + sample invoices)
7) Monitoring plan (SLOs/alerts + owner)
8) Customer/GTM comms required? (Y/N) + links
9) Approvals (Finance/Legal/Eng/Release)

Emily Stone: Looks right. One nuance: for RevRec we need to note whether it’s forward-only or retroactive. Retroactive requires separate approval and a documented materiality assessment.

Laura Bennett: Yes. Default is forward-only. Retroactive adjustment = CFO + GC explicit approval + a customer communication plan.

Logan Wright: On backfills: sometimes we repair missing dimensions (like region) and backfill events. Even if invoice amounts don’t change, it affects analytics + margin. Should that be tracked under the same process?

Emily Stone: Yes, anything that touches the billing datasets used for invoice generation *or* RevRec reporting needs tracking. Even "non-billing" changes can change reported metrics.

Nadia Rahman: We can categorize: (A) invoice-impacting meters, (B) attribution/dimensions only. Both get logged, but (A) has stricter signoff + shadow billing requirement.

Aisha Bello: I can add a "category" tag to the dashboards/alerts too, so we know which alerts are invoice-impacting.

Connor O'Brien: Mechanics: Where do we store the record? Confluence page + link from Linear ticket? Or keep it in the ticket itself?

Sofia Mendes: Confluence is better for audit trail longevity (tickets get messy). But ticket should link to the Confluence record and contain the final approval comments.

Michael Grant: + Confluence. Also include a link to the approved clause library section if the change touches definitions.

Mei Lin: +1. Also, the recon export table schema changes should be documented in Confluence with a changelog.

Talia Benson: Proposed locations:
- Confluence (Finance/Legal > RevRec & Billing): "Meter Change Log" (append-only)
- Each change gets its own subpage with the template above
- Linear ticket (e.g., PM-1872 / ENG-4430) must link to the subpage
- PR(s) must reference the Linear ticket + Confluence subpage in description

Laura Bennett: Make it append-only. No silent edits. If we need to update, add a new entry ("Amendment") with date + reason.

Emily Stone: + add: retain artifacts for X period. What’s reasonable? 7 years is overkill, but we should align to financial audit norms.

Michael Grant: We should align with our data retention policy for billing records. For audit trail docs (not raw usage data), 7 years is common. For snapshots/results, maybe 13 months minimum to cover annual audit + customer disputes.

Sofia Mendes: Dispute window in MSAs is usually 30–60 days, but procurement can push longer. Keeping 13 months is safe.

Logan Wright: For raw shadow-billing output, we can keep aggregated diffs and a pointer to the exact query version. Storing per-request diffs for 7 years isn’t feasible.

Mei Lin: Agree: store (1) the query text/version hash, (2) aggregate result tables exported (CSV) for the comparison window, (3) the set of sampled accounts/days.

Aisha Bello: For dashboard evidence, we can export weekly PDF snapshots for the first month post-change + keep alert history in PagerDuty/Grafana.

Connor O'Brien: Operationally I’ll add a release checklist:
- Shadow billing compare running for >= 7 days
- Drift within threshold (link)
- Finance + Legal approvals captured
- Rollout plan + rollback documented
- Comms planned if customer-facing

Laura Bennett: Perfect. Also, we need to define “threshold” in writing (e.g., drift <0.5% overall and <1% per top-20 account/day), and what happens if it exceeds.

Emily Stone: +1. If it exceeds, we pause rollout and treat it like an incident until reconciled.

Nadia Rahman: Can we treat "meter drift" alerts as Sev-2 for invoice-impacting meters? That way it triggers the right review and creates an incident record.

Aisha Bello: Yes, for invoice-impacting. We can route to Eng + Finance on-call (or at least notify finance_billing).

Talia Benson: Action items:
- Talia: draft Confluence "Meter Change Log" + template page today, share for review
- Connor: add release checklist + label, propose gating rules
- Aisha/Mei: propose evidence retention approach + where artifacts live (Drive vs Confluence attachments)
- Emily/Laura + Michael/Sofia: confirm approval matrix + retention durations

Michael Grant: Please include an explicit note: if the change would materially alter customer charges (even if "fix"), Legal needs to confirm customer notification obligations.

Laura Bennett: Good. Let’s aim to have the template finalized before end of week so the next batch of PRs (retry double-counting, embeddings batch) have a place to land.

Emily Stone: :thumbsup: Everyone ok with Confluence being the source of truth, with links from Linear/PRs? If yes, I’ll reply in the finance_billing thread as the decision.

Sofia Mendes: Yes.

Logan Wright: Yes.

Connor O'Brien: Yes.

Aisha Bello: Yes.

Mei Lin: Yes.

title: #finance: 1770075522
preview:
Laura Bennett: Sharing early shadow-billing compare results (week 1). This is the run that computes both old+new meter outputs but invoices still use current (old) meter. 

Topline from Mei's export (sample: 50 accounts, last 7 days, hosted chat+completions+embeddings):
- Overall delta (new vs old): +0.62% billed_tokens, +0.08% request_count
- P95 account-level delta: +2.4% (two accounts are big outliers)
- Largest negative delta: -3.1% (1 account)

Question: are these expected (token counting alignment) vs bugs (double-count/retries etc)? Need guidance on what we consider acceptable before we expand cohort + turn on alerts.

Mei Lin: Adding more detail. I bucketed deltas by endpoint:
- /v1/chat.completions: +0.4% tokens
- /v1/completions: +0.7% tokens
- /v1/embeddings: +1.9% tokens (but high variance on batched inputs)

Also noticed ~0.3% of usage_events missing region in old pipeline for the sample window; new pipeline has it populated (guessing impacts price mapping in a few cases).

Link to query + table: `snowflake://FINANCE_SANDBOX.SHADOW_BILLING_COMPARE.WK1_DELTA_SUMMARY` (restricted)

Rishi Malhotra: +0.62% overall is not crazy, but the outliers matter (ARPU/margin model is sensitive). Can we list top 5 by absolute $ impact and classify reason?

Emily Stone: +1 on classification. Also reminder: we should not treat shadow deltas as “we owe customers money” unless we can tie to a spec change + approved retro policy. For now I want:
1) identify true bugs (double counting, missing dims)
2) identify spec differences (rounding, streaming semantics)
3) document expected deltas + sign-off

Logan Wright: We’ve seen 2 expected drivers and 1 suspicious one:
Expected:
- OpenAI-compat endpoint token counting alignment (PR-511) can move a few tenths of a percent depending on tool calls + whitespace handling.
- Streaming accounting: old meter sometimes billed on partial chunks in a way that didn’t always reconcile with final usage; new meter does “finalize on completion” unless disconnect (ENG-4410).
Suspicious:
- embeddings batching: I’m worried we’re still double-counting “num_inputs” in a specific path (PR-27569 is supposed to fix but I’m not 100% confident it’s deployed to the shadow job).

Nadia Rahman: For the outliers: can we check if they correlate with retry/fallback? The retry double-count bug (ENG-4430 / PR-27463) was present in late Jan. If shadow compares “new path” w/ fix vs “old path” w/out fix, we’ll see negatives (new < old) for accounts with lots of fallbacks.

Mei Lin: Pulled top 5 by absolute token delta (not $ yet). Quick notes:
1) acct_2198 (Acme AI): new - old = -2.8% tokens. High `fallback_count` and `server_retry_count` in logs.
2) acct_1044: new +7.6% tokens. Mostly embeddings, batch sizes 64–128.
3) acct_3310: new +3.9% tokens. Chat + tool calls, heavy streaming.
4) acct_1187: new +3.2% tokens. Region was null ~4% in old events; new has region.
5) acct_2091: new -1.9% tokens. Also retries.

Cole Summers: If #1/#5 are retries, that’s likely the double-count fix behaving. That would mean old meter was overbilling those requests (bad). But we need to confirm whether those were already invoiced and what we do about it.

Emily Stone: Careful: we need to separate “usage_event truth” vs “invoice ledger truth”. If invoices were computed off the same old logic, we can’t just assume overbilling unless we can reproduce from the ledger inputs. 

Connor O'Brien: Re: deployment: shadow-billing compare job in prod is pinned to meter version `2026.02.01-shadow1`. It includes PR-27518 (shadow compare mode) and PR-27555 (idempotency hardening). It does NOT include PR-27569 (embeddings batch fix) yet; that’s in `shadow2` candidate.

Logan Wright: That explains embeddings variance. We should not interpret embeddings +7% as real until shadow2.

title: #finance: 1770061189
preview:
Emily Stone: Need to lock down the usage data freeze window for month-end close. We keep getting late-arriving usage_events and then the ledger shifts after we’ve already started RevRec. Can we agree on a cutoff + handling for late events/backfills?

Mei Lin: +1. Today we’re kind of doing this ad hoc ("don’t rerun after X"), which is not great for audit.

Logan Wright: Totally. On the telemetry side we have two times:
- event_time (when request happened)
- ingest_time (when it hit the pipeline)
Late events are usually ingest lag or retries/backfill jobs.

Rishi Malhotra: For margin model + forecasting I also need the month to stop moving under us. Even if we true-up later, I need a stable snapshot for close.

Laura Bennett: We should be explicit: what date/time is the close cutoff, what’s the grace period, and what requires approval. Also: if we find a bug, we don’t want silent changes.

Emily Stone: Proposed:
1) Billing month = usage by event_time in UTC (not customer TZ).
2) Close cutoff = T+2 business days at 12:00pm PT (after EOM). After that: no changes to “close snapshot” unless CFO+GC sign-off.
3) Late events after cutoff go to next month with an adjustment/credit memo if material.
Thoughts?

Logan Wright: Using event_time in UTC is consistent w/ what we’re doing, but we should be careful: pushing late events into next month will confuse customers if they’re reconciling daily exports.

Cole Summers: Also, invoices are generated off billing_ledger, not raw usage_events. If we decide a freeze, we should freeze the ledger inputs / snapshot, not just events.

Aisha Bello: From SRE POV we can define an SLO for “freshness” and have alerts if we’re not meeting it before freeze. But we still need a formal “close snapshot” table/version.

Caleb Johnson: Data pipeline reality: 99.5% of Hosted API usage_events arrive within ~4 hours. The tail is from:
- streaming disconnects + retry path
- backfills for missing dimensions
- region tagging fixes
We can likely guarantee “T+1 day 09:00 UTC is 99.9% complete” but 100% is hard without pausing backfills.

Mei Lin: What Finance needs: a stable number for the month. We can tolerate small late trickle as long as it’s documented and doesn’t mutate the already-sent invoice.

Laura Bennett: Exactly. We need an operational rule, not perfection.

Emily Stone: How about this structure:
- Define “Usage Close Snapshot” for month M.
- Snapshot includes all usage_events where event_time in month M and ingest_time <= freeze_time.
- Freeze_time is a single timestamp agreed each month.
- After freeze, events with event_time in M but arriving later are tracked in a “late_events_report” and assessed for materiality.
- Any backfill touching month M after freeze requires Finance approval + ticket + audit notes.

Logan Wright: That works. Engineering can implement:
1) a monthly snapshot job that writes `billing_usage_close_snapshot_vYYYYMM` (or similar) with a run_id
2) a late-events delta query comparing post-freeze arrivals

Cole Summers: Important: we should freeze *pricing logic version* too. If token counting changes mid-month, we should have v1/v2 fields or shadow compare, but invoice should reference the version.

Mei Lin: +1. We should store `meter_version` in the snapshot and in the ledger entry.

Rishi Malhotra: Can we define the freeze in business terms:
- “Final for close” number by a set date
- “True-up” process if we discover meter bugs
That will help me explain to auditors.

Aisha Bello: We can add alerts:
- freshness: max ingest lag p99 < 6h (warn) / < 12h (page) as we approach EOM
- duplicates/idempotency rate thresholds
- drift vs ledger thresholds
(Though drift will depend on the snapshot, not live stream.)

Laura Bennett: Ok, let’s settle on the freeze_time.

Emily Stone: Suggestion: month-end close freeze_time = 2nd business day after month end, 5:00pm PT.
Rationale: gives Eng time for late events and us time for invoice previews.

Logan Wright: That’s workable. We need to coordinate with any planned backfills so they don’t run after freeze for prior month.

Caleb Johnson: 5pm PT on T+2 business day should capture basically all organic late events. Anything beyond that is likely backfill or incident.

Cole Summers: And we can explicitly stop automated backfills for month M at freeze_time. Anything later must be run with a `backfill_reason` and `finance_approval` reference.

Mei Lin: Can we also agree that invoice preview uses the snapshot? (So preview and final are consistent.)

Emily Stone: Yes. Process:
- Run snapshot at freeze_time
- Generate invoice preview from snapshot+pricing tables
- If issues found, open exception ticket; only changes after freeze are via approved exception

Laura Bennett: Great. Materiality threshold?

Rishi Malhotra: For RevRec, I’d propose: if late/backfill delta changes revenue for any single account by >$1k OR total month by >$10k, it triggers CFO review. Smaller than that: record as immaterial and adjust next month if needed.

Emily Stone: Works for me. We can tighten later.

Logan Wright: One nuance: if the late delta is due to a customer-impacting bug (e.g., double-counted retries), we probably don’t want to “adjust next month” silently. We should decide case-by-case w/ Legal/Support.

Laura Bennett: Agree. Default is forward-only adjustments unless customer was overbilled materially or legal requires correction. But it must be explicit.

Mei Lin: Summarizing decision (please confirm):
1) Usage month is based on `event_time` in UTC.
2) Freeze_time for month M = 2nd business day after month end @ 5:00pm PT.
3) We generate a “Usage Close Snapshot” (versioned, includes meter_version) that is the source of truth for invoice preview + close.
4) After freeze_time: no backfills or meter logic changes applied to month M without Finance approval (ticket + audit note).
5) Track post-freeze arrivals as “late_events_report”; evaluate materiality (> $1k/account or > $10k total triggers CFO review).
6) Customer-impacting corrections handled explicitly w/ Finance+Legal+Support (credit memo / adjustment) rather than silent ledger mutation.

Emily Stone: Confirmed.

Laura Bennett: Confirmed. Please turn this into a runbook / checklist item for close.

Aisha Bello: I’ll add an alert/checklist item for “snapshot job ran” + “late event delta” dashboard tile.

Logan Wright: We’ll wire the snapshot job + late delta query into the metering sanity job. Cole/Caleb and I will coordinate.

Cole Summers: I’ll add a guard in the backfill pipeline to require a finance_approval flag for months in “closed” state.

Rishi Malhotra: Thanks all. Once we have the snapshot table, I’ll update the margin model to read from it for close.

Emily Stone: Action items:
- Mei: draft Confluence note + add to month-end close checklist
- Logan/Cole/Caleb: spec snapshot table/run_id/meter_version + implement
- Aisha: alerts/tiles
I’ll follow up in our close channel.

Laura Bennett: :thumbsup:

title: #finance: 1770043123
preview:
Emily Stone: Flagging an invoice discrepancy for Acme AI (Acct: ACME-0147). Feb 1–3 usage export totals are lower than what posted to billing ledger / invoice preview by ~6.8% tokens. Customer already asked for backup detail. Can we triage today? cc @Mei Lin @Nadia Rahman @Logan Wright @Cole Summers

Mei Lin: got it. Do we have the exact invoice lines + their meter dims? model/region? also is this Hosted API or Dedicated too?

Emily Stone: Hosted API only. Invoice line is “Text generation - tokens” (OpenAI-compat endpoints included). No Dedicated line items. Snapshot:
- Invoice preview billed_tokens: 1,982,114
- Customer usage export billed_tokens: 1,854,908
Delta: 127,206 tokens.
They’re on Standard plan, no custom discount. Credits should not apply this period.

Support Bot: Linked support case SUP-28451 ("invoice tokens do not match usage API") created by Support. Requester: Acme AI. Priority: High.

Laura Bennett: 6.8% is too big to hand-wave. Need root cause + decide if we adjust or explain. Please keep an audit trail (queries + screenshots) for revrec.

Nadia Rahman: Hypotheses (based on recent issues):
1) retry/fallback double-counting on streaming path (some of these were fixed but may have been live for Feb 1)
2) late-arriving usage events included in ledger but not in customer export window due to freeze cutoff
3) missing region dimension causing export filter mismatch (if export groups by region)
4) OpenAI-compat token counting mismatch (raw vs billed) if invoice uses billed_tokens_v2 and export still on v1

Logan Wright: +1. We also had a partial outage Feb 2 that triggered server-side retries in us-east. If Acme is mostly us-east, could be correlated.

Mei Lin: I can run a ledger vs usage events recon by day/model/region/account for Feb 1–3. Need Acme’s account_id + invoice_id.

Emily Stone: account_id=acct_9f3c2b, invoice_id=inv_2026_02_acme_ai. Usage export they downloaded is “daily_usage_utc.csv” from Console.

Talia Benson: I’ll track actions/owners here + update SUP ticket once we have a clear narrative.

Cole Summers: quick q: are we sure customer export includes OpenAI-compat endpoints? There’s a toggle in the export query that used to exclude `/v1/chat/completions` for some orgs.

Nadia Rahman: good call. The export uses `usage_api_v1` view (customer-facing). Ledger uses `billing_ledger_v2` which includes openai_compat after Jan 27.
If Acme export is still hitting the old view, that would explain.

Aisha Bello: also check data freshness. There was a backfill job on Feb 3 02:00 UTC for missing cache_hit fields; it can re-emit usage events (should dedupe, but…).

Logan Wright: I’ll check if Acme had duplicate usage_event_ids around that backfill window.

Mei Lin: running query now. Initial results (Feb 1–3):
- drift concentrated in endpoint_type=openai_compat (chat/completions)
- model=rw-llama-70b-instruct mostly
- region=us-east-1 mostly
Numbers:
  Feb 1 delta: +41,980 billed_tokens in ledger vs export
  Feb 2 delta: +63,144
  Feb 3 delta: +22,082
So it’s not just one day.

Cole Summers: that pattern matches export view mismatch more than late events. Late events would skew day boundaries.

Nadia Rahman: can we confirm what the customer export is actually summing? If they’re using raw tokens field or prompt+completion instead of billed_tokens, we’ll see a difference.

Emily Stone: They sent a screenshot (SUP ticket) where their CSV has columns: `prompt_tokens`, `completion_tokens`, `total_tokens`. No `billed_tokens` column in their file.

Laura Bennett: that’s likely the issue then. Invoice is billed_tokens, export is raw tokens. We need to fix the customer export and give Acme a reconciled view ASAP.

Mei Lin: yup. In our internal recon table, billed_tokens = raw_tokens - cache_savings + tool_call_adjustments + rounding. If Acme’s export lacks billed_tokens, they’ll never match.
But note: billed_tokens should usually be <= raw_tokens if caching is on; here invoice is higher than their export, so maybe they’re missing an entire class of events too.

Nadia Rahman: careful: if export is excluding openai_compat entirely, their raw tokens would be *lower* than invoice. That matches what we see.
Let’s validate with a direct compare:
- Sum usage_events where endpoint_type=openai_compat for acct_9f3c2b Feb 1–3
- Compare to non-openai endpoints

Logan Wright: I checked request logs: Acme is 78% traffic via OpenAI-compat `/v1/chat/completions`. So if export excluded those, delta could be exactly this.

Cole Summers: I’ll check config/feature flag for Acme org on export path. There was a legacy “compat_traffic_hidden” flag for early beta.

Mei Lin: query result:
- usage_events (internal) billed_tokens Feb 1–3 all endpoints: 1,981,742 (close to ledger 1,982,114)
- usage_api_v1 export view billed_tokens Feb 1–3: 1,854,620
Difference (127,122) is almost identical to invoice-export delta.
Breakdown of the 127,122:
- 124,9xx tokens are endpoint_type=openai_compat
- remaining ~2.2k are small (rounding + late events)
So export view is excluding openai_compat usage.

Emily Stone: that’s a clean story. Next: customer comms + whether we need to regenerate export or provide a one-off CSV.

Cole Summers: confirmed. Acme org has `usage_export_include_openai_compat=false` from Nov pilot. It should’ve been flipped when we GA’d compat endpoints. I can flip now for future exports.

Nadia Rahman: please don’t just flip silently—needs change control since it changes what customers see (even if it’s more correct). But I think we can do it with a note + support comms.

Laura Bennett: agree. Make sure Support has wording: “export previously omitted OpenAI-compatible endpoints; invoice includes them per MSA usage definition.” Also confirm contract language for Acme doesn’t carve them out.

preview:
Maya Chen: FYI noticed a token surge in yesterday's ledger after delayed ingest — looks like ~1.21M extra tokens processed at ~02:15 UTC, affecting ~119 invoices. Need owners + timeline to correct invoices.
Carlos Rivera: ugh. saw that. pipeline replayed a late batch from S3 that had duplicate events. I'm happy to own the rollback/reconciliation if Priya + finance confirm scope.
Priya Nair: Thanks Carlos. Confirmed duplicates in metering ingestion (duplicate_message_id). Can you share the filter/query you'll use for dedupe? Also need list of affected accounts and provisional credit amounts for finance.
Carlos Rivera: planning to run de-dup job: `python tools/dedupe_tokens.py --after 2026-02-01T02:00Z --before 2026-02-01T03:00Z --key event_id`
Maya Chen: timeline from finance: need provisional credits & estimate by EOD PST, formal invoice corrections in next billing cycle unless urgent.
Jordan Blake: cc @Sam O'Neill, any risk if we revert the replay? Would that impact other downstream reconciliations?
Sam O'Neill: Reverting replay is safest but will re-run downstream aggregations. I'll coordinate; estimate 3-4 hours for full reprocess in staging, 6-8 hrs for prod if we throttle.
Carlos Rivera: ok I'll prepare the dedupe job and a dry-run in staging at 14:00 UTC. Will post logs + diff.
Priya Nair: please include a sample SQL diff: `select account_id, sum(tokens) from usage where timestamp between ... group by account_id` before/after.
Maya Chen: Owners: Carlos = remediation run, Priya = ops validation, Maya = finance approval/credits. Target: dry-run 14:00 UTC, prod run 18:00 UTC, credits file by EOD PST.
Jordan Blake: note recurring pattern: similar delayed replay in Nov — add postmortem action to alert on S3 replays >1hr lag and to require manual approval for replays >10k events.
Maya Chen: added to postmortem action list: `alerts: delayed_replay_threshold`, owner = eng-infra.
Carlos Rivera: posting staging dry-run results in 14:30 UTC. Will tag this thread with results + a small CSV.
billing-bot: Ingest anomaly detected: duplicate_event_rate=0.032, affected_accounts=123, estimated_overcount_tokens=1213000
Maya Chen: thanks — will prepare provisional credits sheet and share with @Jordan and @Priya. If prod run goes ahead, finance to push invoice correction notes.
Carlos Rivera: dry-run results: dedupe delta_tokens=-1,212,984 (expected) — sample diff attached (CSV). Prepping prod run at 18:15 UTC (shifted 15m).
Sam O'Neill: prod run complete at 21:00 UTC. Reconciliation started; initial pass indicates ~1.21M tokens removed, 119 accounts >$100 impact. No downstream data loss.
Maya Chen: closing note: credits prepared, accounting will queue invoice adjustments next cycle unless customers request immediate reissue. Action items captured in postmortem doc: eng-infra to add alert, infra to add replay gating, finance to own invoice adjustments. Thanks everyone :thumbsup:

Priya Natarajan: Fortem Systems AP says their renewal invoice INV-2027-0223 has a duplicate platform fee line and support tier looks off. Can someone triage? @LenaOrtiz @SamirPatel

Lena Ortiz: Pasted the invoice lines from the portal:
```
Invoice INV-2027-0223
Customer: Fortem Systems LLC
Period: 2027-03-01 - 2027-03-31
 - Platform Fee (Private License): $30,000
 - Platform Fee (Committed): $30,000
 - Reserved Capacity: $90,000
 - Support (Standard): $12,000
 - Migration reversal credit: -$10,000
Total: $152,000
```

Samir Patel: Looks like both the private license SKU and the old committed SKU were billed for the same period. Contract AMND-1458 shows they migrated to private license effective 2027-03-01, so only one platform fee should apply. Also they upgraded support to Enterprise effective 2027-02-10, so Standard is wrong.

Support Billing Bot: Invoice INV-2027-0223 generated by billing-pipeline v3.5.0. Automated check: duplicate product_code detected; support-tier mismatch flagged.

Priya Natarajan: Quick math needed for procurement: how much to credit?

Lena Ortiz: Remove duplicate platform line = credit $30,000. Support delta: Enterprise monthly $18,000 vs Standard $12,000 => difference $6,000 underbilled (we owe invoice increase). Migration reversal credit -$10,000 should not be on renewal per AMND-1458 (it applied only once in Feb). So removing that credit increases net owed by $10,000. Net customer-facing change = -30,000 (credit) +6,000 (charge) +10,000 (remove credit) = -14,000 -> we owe customer $14,000.

Samir Patel: Agreed. Recommend issuing credit memo CM-2027-089 for $14,000 and reissuing INV-2027-0223R without the duplicate platform line and with Enterprise support applied. Keep wording neutral.

Eng Billing: Opening ticket BILL-10110 to fix join logic that allowed historical committed SKU to persist after migration; add test to ensure one platform fee per active license at term start.

Support Billing Bot: Ticket BILL-10110 created: "duplicate platform fee after license migration + stale migration credit reapplied". Status: triaged.

Corrected total: $138,000 (CM applied net due $124,000). Uploaded docs to portal and emailed procurement per neutral wording.

title: #finance: 1769311802
preview:
Laura Bennett: hey team — I’m trying to understand how we reconcile the “standard cost model” outputs w/ monthly spend allocation + reporting windows for close.

Laura Bennett: right now we have:
1) cloud invoice + reserved commits (system of record)
2) billing hooks / invoiced usage
3) Console cost breakdown (token attributed)

Laura Bennett: if cost-model API becomes the primitive for 2/3, what is the finance story when we cut the month? (late telemetry, backfills, rollups, overhead allocation changes, etc.)

Rishi Malhotra: +1. the two specific questions:
- what is the “as-of” timestamp semantics? (compute-time vs event-time)
- do we freeze a number for Jan close, or does it keep moving w/ telemetry corrections?

Ethan Park: good qs. cost model v1 is *not* the billing system of record, but we do want finance reporting to use the same primitives so the “why is Console different from invoice” conversations go away.

Ethan Park: on windows: we’re planning to support an explicit reporting window in the API request: `window_start`, `window_end` (UTC) + `as_of`.

Ethan Park: `as_of` is intended to mean “only include telemetry ingested by this time” (so you can run a close snapshot). we’re still aligning on exact naming.

Logan Wright: from telemetry side: we can provide event_time + ingest_time. event_time is when the request happened, ingest_time is when the meter record landed.

Logan Wright: for “freeze”, we typically do something like: window = calendar month by event_time, but apply ingest cutoff (e.g., T+2 days) to avoid forever-moving numbers.

Laura Bennett: ok. so for Jan: we’d do event_time Jan 1-31, ingest cutoff Feb 2 23:59? then anything late gets treated as “prior period true-up”?

Rishi Malhotra: that’s what we do in other parts of the stack, but we need it documented + consistent. also: what about backfills for instrumentation bugs?

Thomas Baird: backfills will exist (esp early on). the proposal is:
- cost model supports replays, but finance view uses “close snapshot id”
- backfills after close should be posted as explicit adjustments, not silently rewriting Jan.

Cole Summers: from billing hooks perspective: invoicing uses our usage ledger. there are also credits/refunds/manual adjustments that won’t show up in pure compute cost.

Cole Summers: so even if cost model provides request-attributed cost, invoice total = usage * price - credits + adjustments. we should be careful w/ language.

Laura Bennett: yeah, totally. I’m not asking to make cost model match invoice line-by-line. I need an allocation basis that *ties out* to actual spend at the month level (or explain deltas cleanly).

Laura Bennett: biggest pain today is: Console shows $X “GPU cost” and cloud bill says $X+20% and we have no stable taxonomy of “overhead vs attributable”

Ethan Park: overhead allocation is addressed in taxonomy ADR (in flight) — v1 output will include:
- request_attributable (prefill/decode)
- request_attributable_cache (savings shown separately)
- platform_overhead_allocated (scheduler waste, idle, control plane, etc.)

Ethan Park: the intended reconciliation is:
SUM(request_attributable + overhead_allocated) ~= actual infra spend for that deployment scope, within a drift threshold.

Rishi Malhotra: can we define “deployment scope” precisely? hosted vs dedicated vs private

Logan Wright: for hosted we can tie to cloud account + cluster tags. dedicated: per-customer pool. private: often customer-owned infra so we don’t want to pretend we know their spend.

Laura Bennett: yes. for private, finance only needs Redwood-side platform costs + licensing revrec. for hosted/dedicated, we need to allocate GPU spend.

Rishi Malhotra: another key: reserved capacity amortization. cloud invoice is lumpy (upfront), but cost model per-request needs a $/GPU-second rate. is that rate fixed monthly? daily? who owns it?

Thomas Baird: we’re planning to publish a “rate card” internally that cost model consumes: effective_date -> blended $/GPU-second by SKU/region/deployment.

Thomas Baird: finance can own the rate card inputs (commit amortization + on-demand + spot blending). cost model just applies the rate deterministically.

Laura Bennett: that’s good. I want deterministic + auditable. also want “which rate card version did you use” in the output.

Ethan Park: yep. we’ll include `rate_card_version` and `overhead_policy_version` fields in responses.

Cole Summers: and similarly `schema_version` / `cost_model_version` so when Optimize runs a what-if, it can cite which version generated the projection.

Rishi Malhotra: reporting windows: do we do UTC months or customer-local? finance is UTC.

Laura Bennett: UTC for close. customer-facing in Console can be local-time but needs to be clear.

Ethan Park: agree. API will be UTC only; UI can translate. (we’ve gotten burned by local time rollups.)

Logan Wright: note: telemetry late-arrival distribution: p99 within ~6 hours, but occasionally 24-48h during outages. so a T+2 day cutoff is pretty safe.

Laura Bennett: what’s the plan for a telemetry outage near month-end? do we have an incident runbook / explicit status so finance knows snapshots are incomplete?

Thomas Baird: drift job + completeness metrics will be on the cost-model dashboards. we can add a “data completeness SLI” per day.

Ethan Park: we should also document a close checklist: (1) check telemetry lag, (2) check drift %, (3) run snapshot job, (4) publish snapshot id.

Talia Benson: I can capture that as an action item. sounds like we need a short finance-facing doc that spells out:
- definitions (event_time vs ingest_time)
- month-end cutoff policy
- backfill / true-up policy
- how overhead is allocated + versioned
- how reserved commits are turned into a blended rate

Laura Bennett: yes please. and can we explicitly call out expected deltas vs invoice? (credits, refunds, pricing promos, support fees)

Cole Summers: + taxes too (in some regions). cost model should probably expose “compute cost”, not “invoice amount”.

Rishi Malhotra: last question: can we get a stable export for FP&A (daily table) instead of hitting the API ad-hoc?

Ethan Park: long term yes. near term we can have a scheduled job that writes “finance_cost_snapshot_daily” with the same primitives. the API is for Console/Optimize; finance can use the snapshot table.

Laura Bennett: ok. next steps?

Talia Benson: action items:

- Laura/Rishi: confirm month-end policy (UTC window + T+2 cutoff + explicit adjustments)

text:
Laura Bennett: hey team — I’m trying to understand how we reconcile the “standard cost model” outputs w/ monthly spend allocation + reporting windows for close.

Laura Bennett: right now we have:
1) cloud invoice + reserved commits (system of record)
2) billing hooks / invoiced usage
3) Console cost breakdown (token attributed)

Laura Bennett: if cost-model API becomes the primitive for 2/3, what is the finance story when we cut the month? (late telemetry, backfills, rollups, overhead allocation changes, etc.)

Rishi Malhotra: +1. the two specific questions:
- what is the “as-of” timestamp semantics? (compute-time vs event-time)
- do we freeze a number for Jan close, or does it keep moving w/ telemetry corrections?

Ethan Park: good qs. cost model v1 is *not* the billing system of record, but we do want finance reporting to use the same primitives so the “why is Console different from invoice” conversations go away.

Ethan Park: on windows: we’re planning to support an explicit reporting window in the API request: `window_start`, `window_end` (UTC) + `as_of`.

Ethan Park: `as_of` is intended to mean “only include telemetry ingested by this time” (so you can run a close snapshot). we’re still aligning on exact naming.

Logan Wright: from telemetry side: we can provide event_time + ingest_time. event_time is when the request happened, ingest_time is when the meter record landed.

Logan Wright: for “freeze”, we typically do something like: window = calendar month by event_time, but apply ingest cutoff (e.g., T+2 days) to avoid forever-moving numbers.

Laura Bennett: ok. so for Jan: we’d do event_time Jan 1-31, ingest cutoff Feb 2 23:59? then anything late gets treated as “prior period true-up”?

Rishi Malhotra: that’s what we do in other parts of the stack, but we need it documented + consistent. also: what about backfills for instrumentation bugs?

Thomas Baird: backfills will exist (esp early on). the proposal is:
- cost model supports replays, but finance view uses “close snapshot id”
- backfills after close should be posted as explicit adjustments, not silently rewriting Jan.

Cole Summers: from billing hooks perspective: invoicing uses our usage ledger. there are also credits/refunds/manual adjustments that won’t show up in pure compute cost.

Cole Summers: so even if cost model provides request-attributed cost, invoice total = usage * price - credits + adjustments. we should be careful w/ language.

Laura Bennett: yeah, totally. I’m not asking to make cost model match invoice line-by-line. I need an allocation basis that *ties out* to actual spend at the month level (or explain deltas cleanly).

Laura Bennett: biggest pain today is: Console shows $X “GPU cost” and cloud bill says $X+20% and we have no stable taxonomy of “overhead vs attributable”

Ethan Park: overhead allocation is addressed in taxonomy ADR (in flight) — v1 output will include:
- request_attributable (prefill/decode)
- request_attributable_cache (savings shown separately)
- platform_overhead_allocated (scheduler waste, idle, control plane, etc.)

Ethan Park: the intended reconciliation is:
SUM(request_attributable + overhead_allocated) ~= actual infra spend for that deployment scope, within a drift threshold.

Rishi Malhotra: can we define “deployment scope” precisely? hosted vs dedicated vs private

Logan Wright: for hosted we can tie to cloud account + cluster tags. dedicated: per-customer pool. private: often customer-owned infra so we don’t want to pretend we know their spend.

Laura Bennett: yes. for private, finance only needs Redwood-side platform costs + licensing revrec. for hosted/dedicated, we need to allocate GPU spend.

Rishi Malhotra: another key: reserved capacity amortization. cloud invoice is lumpy (upfront), but cost model per-request needs a $/GPU-second rate. is that rate fixed monthly? daily? who owns it?

Thomas Baird: we’re planning to publish a “rate card” internally that cost model consumes: effective_date -> blended $/GPU-second by SKU/region/deployment.

Thomas Baird: finance can own the rate card inputs (commit amortization + on-demand + spot blending). cost model just applies the rate deterministically.

Laura Bennett: that’s good. I want deterministic + auditable. also want “which rate card version did you use” in the output.

Ethan Park: yep. we’ll include `rate_card_version` and `overhead_policy_version` fields in responses.

Cole Summers: and similarly `schema_version` / `cost_model_version` so when Optimize runs a what-if, it can cite which version generated the projection.

Rishi Malhotra: reporting windows: do we do UTC months or customer-local? finance is UTC.

Laura Bennett: UTC for close. customer-facing in Console can be local-time but needs to be clear.

Ethan Park: agree. API will be UTC only; UI can translate. (we’ve gotten burned by local time rollups.)

Logan Wright: note: telemetry late-arrival distribution: p99 within ~6 hours, but occasionally 24-48h during outages. so a T+2 day cutoff is pretty safe.

Laura Bennett: what’s the plan for a telemetry outage near month-end? do we have an incident runbook / explicit status so finance knows snapshots are incomplete?

Thomas Baird: drift job + completeness metrics will be on the cost-model dashboards. we can add a “data completeness SLI” per day.

Ethan Park: we should also document a close checklist: (1) check telemetry lag, (2) check drift %, (3) run snapshot job, (4) publish snapshot id.

Talia Benson: I can capture that as an action item. sounds like we need a short finance-facing doc that spells out:
- definitions (event_time vs ingest_time)
- month-end cutoff policy
- backfill / true-up policy
- how overhead is allocated + versioned
- how reserved commits are turned into a blended rate

Laura Bennett: yes please. and can we explicitly call out expected deltas vs invoice? (credits, refunds, pricing promos, support fees)

Cole Summers: + taxes too (in some regions). cost model should probably expose “compute cost”, not “invoice amount”.

Rishi Malhotra: last question: can we get a stable export for FP&A (daily table) instead of hitting the API ad-hoc?

Ethan Park: long term yes. near term we can have a scheduled job that writes “finance_cost_snapshot_daily” with the same primitives. the API is for Console/Optimize; finance can use the snapshot table.

Laura Bennett: ok. next steps?

Talia Benson: action items:
- Ethan/Thomas: propose the `as_of` + snapshot semantics and include version fields (rate card + overhead policy) in v1 response
- Logan: share telemetry late-arrival stats + recommended close cutoff
- Cole: list non-compute invoice components we should document as expected reconciliation deltas
- Laura/Rishi: confirm month-end policy (UTC window + T+2 cutoff + explicit adjustments)

Laura Bennett: :thumbsup: thanks all. I’ll wait for the doc + we’ll review before we bless this for Q1 close.

Ethan Park: sounds good. will loop back once the finance-alignment doc is drafted.

Anna Chen: FYI — VelaHealth renewal invoice looks wrong. Platform fee prorated to 19 days and support billed as Standard, but contract has Enterprise support starting Feb 10. Attaching invoice snippet. @MikeGarcia @PriyaNatarajan can you take a look?

```
Invoice ID: INV-2026-0419
Customer: VelaHealth Inc.
Term start: 2026-03-01
Line items:
  - Platform Fee (private deployment): $10,800 (prorated, 19/30 days)
  - Reserved Capacity: $45,000
  - Support (Standard): $9,000
  - One-time migration credit: -$5,000
Total: $59,800
```

Priya Natarajan: This is the on-prem private license renewal, right? They upgraded to Enterprise support on 2026-02-10 per change order. Renewal date of Mar 1 should bill enterprise tier prorate from Feb10–Mar1, then full month thereafter. Looks like our billing applied Standard incorrectly.

Support Billing Bot: Invoice INV-2026-0419 (https://billing.redwood.ai/invoices/INV-2026-0419) generated by billing-pipeline v3.2.1 — attached PDF.

Jordan Lee: If this is an internal billing error we should issue a credit memo and reissue the renewal invoice. Legal prefers credits for clear mistakes rather than adjustments buried in next month. Keep a record.

Mike Garcia: Pulling contract 6543-PL now. Quick check — platform fee is a flat monthly $18,000 (no daily proration clause except at initial term). I suspect the migration credit (-$5,000) got reapplied at renewal, and the prorate logic in the pipeline used the migration start date instead of term start.

Anna Chen: Can you run numbers? Need estimate of the correction to tell the customer.

Mike Garcia: Quick math: they were invoiced platform $10,800 (19/30 * 18,000). They should be charged full $18,000 for the renewal. Delta = $7,200. For support: Enterprise monthly is $13,500; invoiced Standard $9,000 so underbilled $4,500 for the first month. Total shortfall = $11,700. If we also remove the migration credit that was incorrectly applied at renewal, that restores $5,000 back to invoice, but since migration credit should have been expired, we need to check its lifecycle. Net customer-facing credit to issue = $11,700 (we owe them that).

Priya Natarajan: Agreed. Customer asked procurement for a corrected invoice and explanation. I'd recommend issuing a credit memo for $11,700, reissuing the corrected renewal invoice (without the migration credit), and send an explanatory note.

Jordan Lee: Approved to issue credit memo. Use credit-memo template and include sentence: "Issued to correct billing error related to platform fee proration and support tier on renewal." No admission beyond that.

Anna Chen: I'll prepare the credit memo and updated invoice. Plan: (1) create credit memo $11,700, (2) reissue INV-2026-0419 as INV-2026-0419R with corrected lines, (3) notify customer and procurement with both docs. Assigning to @MikeGarcia to open a billing bug and to @Finance Ops to publish credit. ETA: EOD Friday.

Mike Garcia: :thumbsup: Opening internal bug now and pinging billing infra for a hotfix. I'll include tests so migration credits aren't reapplied at renewal. Ticket: BILL-9921.

Support Billing Bot: Ticket BILL-9921 created: "renewal proration + migration credit misapplied". Current status: triaged.

Finance Ops: Noted — will prepare credit memo template and queue for approval once Anna uploads the calc.

Anna Chen: Draft message to customer for review — please suggest wording:

"Hi <Procurement Contact>,
We identified an error on your recent renewal invoice INV-2026-0419 relating to platform fee proration and support tier application. We will issue a credit memo for $11,700 and reissue the corrected invoice within 3 business days. We apologize for the confusion and will expedite this for your procurement process. Please let me know if you need any additional documentation.
Best,
Anna Chen (Finance)"

Priya Natarajan: I'd add: "We appreciate your patience; procurement questions can be directed to myself or Anna." Also remove the word "error"? Customer prefers neutral phrasing sometimes. Maybe: "We identified an inconsistency on the invoice and have corrected it."

Anna Chen: Good call — changing wording now. Final draft: "We identified an inconsistency on your renewal invoice and have issued a credit memo for $11,700. A corrected invoice will be reissued within 3 business days. We apologize for the disruption and are available for any follow-up for procurement."

Mike Garcia: I'll follow up on BILL-9921 and provide ETA for fix.

Anna Chen: Thanks everyone. I'll close loop after credit issued and invoice reissued, then update the accounting records and revenue true-up.

Priya Natarajan: Thanks — looping in customer success to proactively message the customer afterwards.

Finance Ops: Journal entries done, credit applied. Marking as resolved.

title: #finance: 1738720044
preview:
Laura Bennett: Flagging where Finance is for signoff on Unit Economics dashboard v1. We’re close, but I can’t sign off until these blockers are cleared:
1) Reconciliation vs billing totals (hosted + dedicated). Need a documented reconciliation report + known deltas.
2) Rate card provenance + versioning. I need the source of truth, update cadence, and what version was used for any reporting period.
3) Idle capacity / shared overhead allocation. Need explicit treatment + what’s included/excluded.

Target: I’d like to sign off by EOD Tue 2/11 so this can be used in the FY26 KPI pack draft.

@Rishi Malhotra can you confirm methodology deltas vs FP&A? @Logan Wright @Mei Lin can you confirm when recon view is stable?

Rishi Malhotra: +1. For (2) specifically: what’s the rate card table + how do we guarantee that “Jan 2025” numbers don’t change when we update the Q4 2025 rate card? We need “as-of” behavior or explicit restatement policy.

Talia Benson: Thanks. I’ll track as blockers w/ owners. Current status from last sync:
- Reconciliation report: ENG-8430 in progress (Mei / Cole)
- Rate card: Finance doc exists (gpu-cost-rate-card-2025q4) but pipeline needs version pin
- Idle/overhead: ADR-034 drafted but needs Finance review + dashboard callout

Logan Wright: On recon: we have a draft view comparing aggregates to billing-export totals. Still seeing ~1.8–2.4% variance depending on day boundary + streaming mix.
Biggest drivers we’ve identified:
- late-arriving metering events (hourly pipeline)
- streaming output tokens undercount on older builds (fixed post PR-19355, but backfill still pending)
- timezone boundary mismatch (Finance uses PT day, pipeline uses UTC hour buckets)

Mei Lin: +1. I can share the current recon query + dashboard screenshot, but I don’t want Finance to treat it as final until we lock the timezone handling. We can support PT reporting in the “finance view” w/ fixed offsets, but it will be separate from the “live ops” view.

Laura Bennett: The variance is fine if it’s consistent + explained, but I need:
- A written “expected deltas” section
- A threshold for when it’s an incident vs normal noise
- A process for “close” (e.g., QTD numbers are frozen after N days)

Cole Summers: For (3) idle/overhead: current ADR proposes allocating idle GPU cost pro-rata on *consumed tokens* by (region, tier, model family) for the same window. Shared overhead (SRE oncall, control plane) allocated as a flat % uplift applied after infra cost.
Open question: do we allocate idle to dedicated/private the same as hosted? If dedicated has reserved capacity, idle is “owned” by that contract in our books.

Rishi Malhotra: Re idle: from FP&A perspective:
- Dedicated reserved capacity idle should stay within Dedicated tier (otherwise you’re subsidizing hosted).
- Hosted pool idle can be blended across hosted usage.
- Private: depends on deal structure; most are platform fee + customer-managed infra so infra idle should be ~0 in our spend model. (But if we’re hosting Private in our VPC, it behaves like dedicated.)
So the tier rules need to be explicit.

Dylan Brooks: Agree. We can implement tier-specific idle allocation:
- hosted: idle allocated across hosted
- dedicated: idle allocated within dedicated
- private: depends on deployment mode; we should encode “private_hosted_by_redwood=true” vs “customer_managed=true” in the cost inputs

Sean Gallagher: From SRE side, if the definition depends on tier flags + cost inputs, we need data quality checks:
- missing tier mapping rate
- sudden shift in tier distribution
- region=unknown spikes
Otherwise recon will drift and we’ll get paged by Finance at close :)

Laura Bennett: Fine by me, but keep the dashboard logic simple. Finance needs a stable KPI, not a science project.

Rishi Malhotra: For (2) rate card provenance: can we confirm the exact source file + owner? Also: does the pipeline ingest “effective_start/end” dates? I want to avoid silent retroactive changes.

Mei Lin: Today we ingest the sheet manually into a warehouse table (finance.gpu_rate_card) with fields:
- provider
- region
- sku
- hourly_cost_usd
- capacity_type (on_demand/reserved)
But we’re missing:
- rate_card_version
- effective_start_ts / effective_end_ts
We can add version + effective dates; then aggregation job joins on event_ts between effective ranges.

Logan Wright: That’s doable. We also need to decide restatement policy: do we recompute historical costs when rate card changes? My preference:
- keep “finance” views pinned to a version for a quarter
- “live ops” can always use latest to catch regressions quickly

Laura Bennett: Yes. For exec reporting we cannot have numbers change week-over-week because we updated a rate card assumption.
Proposal:
- Quarterly: pin rate card version at quarter start; restate only with CFO approval.
- Monthly close: freeze after T+5 business days; late metering can land but we flag it and roll into next month if outside window.

Talia Benson: Capturing decisions:
- Add rate_card_version + effective dates; finance view pins version by reporting period
- Tier-specific idle allocation (hosted vs dedicated vs private modes)
- Two views: “live ops” (freshness) vs “finance” (stability)

Can we get owners + dates?

Cole Summers: I can update ADR-034 w/ tier idle rules + overhead uplift definition by Fri 2/7.

Mei Lin: I can update the rate card ingestion schema + backfill the table with version tags by Mon 2/10.

Logan Wright: Reconciliation report: we can get the variance down and document remaining deltas. Target Mon 2/10 for a “finance-ready” recon dashboard + notes.

Sean Gallagher: I’ll add alert thresholds for:
- missing hours > 1
- token volume drop > 20% vs trailing 7d baseline
- unknown tier/region > 0.5%
by Tue 2/11.

Rishi Malhotra: One more blocker: inclusion/exclusion rules. Are we including:
- embeddings?
- rerank?
- internal eval traffic?
For blended $/1M output tokens KPI, I assume only text/chat generation output tokens, excluding eval + test.

Logan Wright: Correct: v1 KPI is text/chat output tokens only. Embeddings/rerank excluded from denominator. Costs: we include GPU serving costs + a % overhead uplift per ADR. Internal eval traffic excluded from both tokens and cost where tagged (still have some tag gaps; we’ll quantify).

Laura Bennett: Great. Please put that in the methodology doc + in-dashboard tooltip. And I need explicit “known limitations” in v1 (streaming parity history, tag gaps, etc.).

Talia Benson: Action items recap:
- @Cole Summers update ADR-034 + post for Finance review (Fri 2/7)
- @Mei Lin rate card versioning + effective dates in warehouse (Mon 2/10)
- @Logan Wright recon report + documented deltas/thresholds (Mon 2/10)
- @Sean Gallagher SLO/alerts + incident criteria (Tue 2/11)
- @Rishi Malhotra + @Laura Bennett review + signoff meeting (Tue 2/11 3pm PT)

Laura Bennett: Works. If recon is within ~2% and explained + rate card versioning is in place, I’m comfortable signing off 2/11.

Rishi Malhotra: Same. Please make sure the dashboard shows the rate_card_version used for the selected time range (even if it’s just in a footer).

Mei Lin: Will do. I’ll add it to the response payload and UI can display “Assumptions: GPU rate card 2025Q4 v3; overhead uplift 7%” etc.

Dylan Brooks: Thanks all. We’ll keep the “live ops” view separate so Eng can move fast without breaking Finance reporting.

Laura Bennett: Appreciate it. Keep this thread updated when the artifacts are ready for review.
