title: #finance: 1738020100
preview:
Rishi Malhotra: hey all — quick question as we get closer to wiring the Unit Economics dashboard to the GPU rate card. How often are we planning to update the rate card assumptions, and how are we versioning those updates so Finance can do QTD/QoQ reporting without the numbers changing under us?

Cole Summers: +1. from the pipeline side we can ingest a new rate table whenever Finance publishes it, but we need a clear contract: update cadence + whether we ever restate historical periods.

Mei Lin: for the aggregation job: we join usage(hourly tokens) to cost rates by `region_normalized` + `capacity_type` + `gpu_family` (where we have it) and apply a `rate_effective_at` window. If the rate changes, it will affect all downstream materializations unless we pin.

Laura Bennett: Finance preference: stable methodology within a quarter. If we update assumptions mid-quarter, we need to be able to say “FY26 Q1 reported with rate card vX” and keep that frozen for the KPI pack.

Logan Wright: we also have engineering reality: cloud provider pricing / reserved commits change and we want the dashboard to reflect reality for optimization work. can we do both? (live view + finance view)

Rishi Malhotra: exactly. I’m fine with a “live” view for Eng as long as the Finance/reporting view is versioned and reproducible.

Allison Grant: from observability side: alerting will be on data freshness/completeness, but rate-card changes will look like a step function in $/1M output tokens. we should annotate rate-card version changes so people don’t page / file incidents for expected shifts.

Cole Summers: proposal:
- Rate card has explicit `rate_card_version` (e.g. `2025Q4_v1`, `2025Q4_v2`)
- Each row has `valid_from` and optional `valid_to`
- Aggregation stores the `applied_rate_card_version` per hourly rollup partition
- Dashboard shows the applied version + allows filtering to a pinned version

Mei Lin: + for implementation: if we store `applied_rate_card_version` in the fact table, it becomes a dimension we can group/filter on without reprocessing.

Laura Bennett: cadence wise, what are you assuming? monthly? quarterly?

Rishi Malhotra: my preference: quarterly baseline (aligned to close), with rare intra-quarter updates only when there’s a material change or we discover an error.

Logan Wright: “rare” is subjective 😅 but workable. if we do monthly, we’ll be chasing it forever. quarterly is easier for comms.

Cole Summers: if we do quarterly baseline, we should still support “effective month” within the quarter (e.g., new reserved commit begins Feb 15). do we need that precision?

Laura Bennett: for KPI reporting, we don’t need mid-month precision. for internal optimization, it might be nice but not required for v1. If there’s a big change mid-quarter, we can capture it in the next quarter’s card or call it out.

Mei Lin: ok so we can define:
- default update cadence = quarterly
- effective boundary = quarter start (00:00 UTC?)
- optional hotfix = new version with same quarter label but incremented suffix

Allison Grant: please not 00:00 UTC if Finance reports in PT. we’ve already had day-boundary confusion in other rollups.

Rishi Malhotra: yes, for Finance reporting we should align to Finance day boundary (PT). For hourly data we can keep UTC but the “effective date” on rate cards should be unambiguous.

Talia Benson: jumping in — can we capture a clear decision + ownership? Otherwise we’ll keep re-litigating this every time the dashboard changes.

Cole Summers: decision draft:
1) Rate card updates on a quarterly cadence (published by FP&A).
2) Rate card is versioned; version changes are append-only (no in-place edits).
3) Historical restatements: only via publishing a new version. Default dashboards:
   - “Live” view uses latest version.
   - “Finance” view pins to Finance-approved version for the quarter.
4) Aggregation persists `applied_rate_card_version` so results are reproducible.
5) Any version change requires annotation in dashboard + Slack heads-up in #finance + #eng-platform.

Laura Bennett: I like it, with one more explicit rule: we don’t restate already-closed quarters for executive reporting unless there’s an error > threshold (say >2% impact). Otherwise, it’s chaos.

Rishi Malhotra: +1. and for errors, we need a reconciliation note. also please make the dashboard display the rate card provenance (link to the Drive doc) so auditors / CFO staff can trace it.

Mei Lin: we can include `rate_card_source_url` as a string column in the dimension table and surface it via the API.

Logan Wright: question: if live view uses latest version, do we backfill the materialized views when a new version lands? or do we recompute on query?

Cole Summers: backfill. query-time recompute would be too heavy + would break our caching assumptions. We can run an idempotent re-aggregation for affected date ranges when a new version is published.

Allison Grant: if we’re backfilling, we need guardrails so a backfill doesn’t take down the warehouse (we just had a near-miss). schedule in a window + throttle.

Mei Lin: yep. can restrict to off-peak and chunk by day. also we can have the finance view point at a “pinned” schema/table that we only refresh when Finance approves.

Laura Bennett: ok. action items:
- Rishi: define what “material error threshold” is + the signoff flow for a new version.
- Cole/Mei: add `rate_card_version` + `source_url` fields, and ensure the dashboard shows version + effective date.
- Logan: confirm whether the v1 API supports selecting rate_card_version or if that’s v1.1.

Rishi Malhotra: I’ll draft the signoff checklist and share in this thread. target: by EOW.

Logan Wright: v1 API can include it as a filter (even if UI hides initially). safer for debugging.

Talia Benson: I’ll log this as a decision in the project notes and make sure it’s reflected in the PRD/ADR references.

Allison Grant: and please ping me when we know what the UI annotation looks like. we can suppress alert noise when a rate-card change is expected.

Laura Bennett: thanks all. this is the kind of boring rigor that keeps us out of trouble later.

Laura Bennett: for KPI reporting, we don’t need mid-month precision. for internal optimization, it might be nice but not required for v1. If there’s a big change mid-quarter, we can capture it in the next quarter’s card or call it out.

Mei Lin: ok so we can define:
- default update cadence = quarterly
- effective boundary = quarter start (00:00 UTC?)
- optional hotfix = new version with same quarter label but incremented suffix

Allison Grant: please not 00:00 UTC if Finance reports in PT. we’ve already had day-boundary confusion in other rollups.

Rishi Malhotra: yes, for Finance reporting we should align to Finance day boundary (PT). For hourly data we can keep UTC but the “effective date” on rate cards should be unambiguous.

Talia Benson: jumping in — can we capture a clear decision + ownership? Otherwise we’ll keep re-litigating this every time the dashboard changes.

Cole Summers: decision draft:
1) Rate card updates on a quarterly cadence (published by FP&A).
2) Rate card is versioned; version changes are append-only (no in-place edits).
3) Historical restatements: only via publishing a new version. Default dashboards:
   - “Live” view uses latest version.
   - “Finance” view pins to Finance-approved version for the quarter.
4) Aggregation persists `applied_rate_card_version` so results are reproducible.
5) Any version change requires annotation in dashboard + Slack heads-up in #finance + #eng-platform.

Laura Bennett: I like it, with one more explicit rule: we don’t restate already-closed quarters for executive reporting unless there’s an error > threshold (say >2% impact). Otherwise, it’s chaos.

Rishi Malhotra: +1. and for errors, we need a reconciliation note. also please make the dashboard display the rate card provenance (link to the Drive doc) so auditors / CFO staff can trace it.

Mei Lin: we can include `rate_card_source_url` as a string column in the dimension table and surface it via the API.

Logan Wright: question: if live view uses latest version, do we backfill the materialized views when a new version lands? or do we recompute on query?

Cole Summers: backfill. query-time recompute would be too heavy + would break our caching assumptions. We can run an idempotent re-aggregation for affected date ranges when a new version is published.

Allison Grant: if we’re backfilling, we need guardrails so a backfill doesn’t take down the warehouse (we just had a near-miss). schedule in a window + throttle.

Mei Lin: yep. can restrict to off-peak and chunk by day. also we can have the finance view point at a “pinned” schema/table that we only refresh when Finance approves.

Laura Bennett: ok. action items:
- Rishi: define what “material error threshold” is + the signoff flow for a new version.
- Cole/Mei: add `rate_card_version` + `source_url` fields, and ensure the dashboard shows version + effective date.
- Logan: confirm whether the v1 API supports selecting rate_card_version or if that’s v1.1.

Rishi Malhotra: I’ll draft the signoff checklist and share in this thread. target: by EOW.

Logan Wright: v1 API can include it as a filter (even if UI hides initially). safer for debugging.

Talia Benson: I’ll log this as a decision in the project notes and make sure it’s reflected in the PRD/ADR references.

Allison Grant: and please ping me when we know what the UI annotation looks like. we can suppress alert noise when a rate-card change is expected.

Laura Bennett: thanks all. this is the kind of boring rigor that keeps us out of trouble later.

title: #finance: 1737334870
preview:
Rishi Malhotra: Hey all — FP&A ask for the unit economics dashboard: we need **quarter-to-date blended $/1M output tokens** for the exec KPI pack.

Rishi Malhotra: A few questions we need to lock down so we don’t get surprised during close:
1) What’s the definition of **QTD window** (calendar quarter vs fiscal)?
2) What’s the **day boundary / timezone**? We report in PT at day granularity.
3) What’s the **freshness expectation**? If we’re using hourly rollups, when do we consider a day “final”?
4) **Reconciliation expectations**: what % delta vs billing/infra spend is acceptable and where do we document known gaps?

Laura Bennett: +1. We can’t put a metric in the KPI pack unless we can explain deltas. Even if it’s “this is allocation-based, not invoice-based” — needs to be explicit.

Talia Benson: Looping in telemetry + product folks. @Logan Wright @Mei Lin @Nadia Rahman @Marissa Cole — can you weigh in on QTD definition + reconciliation plan?

Logan Wright: For QTD window: product asked for **calendar quarter** (Q1 = Jan 1) for now. If Finance needs fiscal quarter mapping we can add a “fiscal_calendar_id” later, but v1 is calendar.

Rishi Malhotra: Calendar quarter is fine for exec pack (we can translate separately), but we need consistent boundary logic.

Mei Lin: On timezone/day boundary: the warehouse tables are currently partitioned by `hour_utc`. The dashboard can do QTD by filtering on UTC hours, but to match Finance PT day boundary we should either:
- convert to PT in the query layer and define QTD as `>= 2025-01-01 00:00:00 PT`, or
- keep UTC and accept day-boundary mismatch (not ideal).

Mei Lin: I strongly recommend we add a standard “reporting_timezone” option. For Finance view we can hardcode PT.

Laura Bennett: Please don’t make us explain why Jan 1 numbers look “off by a day” every quarter. Hardcode PT for finance view is fine.

Marissa Cole: From Console side we can support a “Finance view” with PT day rollups + a “Live view” that’s hour_utc. We were already debating this for caching TTL vs freshness.

Nadia Rahman: Freshness: the hourly aggregation job runs at ~T+45m on average, but we’ve seen occasional ingestion lags up to 2–3h when the gateway pipeline backpressures.

Nadia Rahman: Suggestion: for Finance reporting, we define a **daily close watermark**:
- “Yesterday” is considered final at **09:00 PT** (or similar) once completeness checks pass.
- Anything after that is “prelim” for intraday monitoring.

Rishi Malhotra: That works. For QTD, we can consume “finalized through yesterday” for KPI pack.

Laura Bennett: Reconciliation: we need a stated expectation. If we allocate idle and shared overhead, it will *never* match billing exactly.

Logan Wright: Right now the blended metric is built from:
- numerator: allocated GPU cost (rate card x GPU-hours) + shared overhead allocation
- denominator: metered output tokens

Logan Wright: It should reconcile to **infra spend inputs** used in the rate card model over the same window, not to customer invoice totals. Invoice totals include margin, discounts, credits, etc.

Rishi Malhotra: Yes, but we still need a sanity check vs invoice volume (e.g., tokens) and a line for expected differences.

Mei Lin: We’re planning a reconciliation view that compares:
A) dashboard numerator (allocated cost)
B) raw cost input totals by region/provider (same rate-card version)
C) billing token totals (output tokens) by tier

Mei Lin: Biggest sources of delta we’ve seen in test runs:
- missing output tokens on streaming paths (fix in progress)
- region normalization dropping rows when region is null/unknown
- customer tier mapping changes (backfills)

Nadia Rahman: On the streaming gap: until the parity fix is fully rolled out, QTD will undercount output tokens for streaming-heavy routes, inflating $/1M output tokens. We can add a “coverage/confidence” indicator and a note in the finance view.

Laura Bennett: We can live with caveats early on, but for KPI pack we need a go/no-go rule. Example: “only publish if token completeness >= 99% and streaming parity enabled in all regions.”

Marissa Cole: That’s consistent with what we’re planning: show a small “Data quality” callout with status (green/yellow/red) and link to the reconciliation report + runbook.

Rishi Malhotra: Can we define an acceptable delta threshold? For example:
- Cost numerator: within **±2%** of cost input totals (same window)
- Tokens denominator: within **±1%** vs metering source-of-truth totals

Rishi Malhotra: If outside, dashboard should flag “not finance-ready”.

Logan Wright: Yes, but we need to be careful: some deltas are expected (e.g., we intentionally exclude non-LLM GPU workloads in cost inputs, depending on tagging accuracy). I’d rather define checks as:
- % of cost inputs successfully mapped to canonical region + capacity type
- % of token events with required dimensions (model, region, tier)

Logan Wright: Then a derived “finance-ready” boolean when all checks pass thresholds.

Laura Bennett: That’s fine. But we still want a simple number in the exec pack with “as of” date and a footnote.

Talia Benson: Action items I’m hearing:
1) Define Finance QTD as **calendar quarter** + **PT day boundary**.
2) Implement “Finance view” daily rollup with watermark (yesterday final at ~09:00 PT) and a “Live view” for hourly.
3) Add reconciliation report + explicit expected differences (allocation-based vs invoice-based).
4) Add data quality gates for “finance-ready” publishing.

Talia Benson: @Rishi Malhotra @Laura Bennett can you propose the exact watermark time + thresholds you’re comfortable with? @Logan Wright @Mei Lin can you confirm feasibility for v1?

Rishi Malhotra: Watermark: 09:00 PT is good. Thresholds draft:
- Dimension completeness (model/region/tier/streaming flag/quant profile): >= 99%
- Token volume delta vs metering totals: <= 1%
- Cost mapping coverage (rate card join success): >= 99.5%

Laura Bennett: Works. Also please version the rate card used. If rate card changes mid-quarter, QTD needs to either:
- lock to a version for the quarter (reporting stability), or
- re-state history (but then exec pack shifts retroactively).

Mei Lin: We can store `rate_card_version` in the aggregated tables and allow Finance view to default to “latest approved for quarter” (locked) while Live view uses latest available.

Logan Wright: +1. That aligns with the ADR direction (versioned attribution model inputs).

Nadia Rahman: If we lock rate card version for Finance view, we should also lock any tier mapping version for the quarter (or at least log when mapping rules change). Otherwise reconciliation will drift.

Marissa Cole: Product-wise we can add a small “Methodology version” label in the UI (rate card version + attribution model version). Keeps everyone honest.

Rishi Malhotra: Great. Please make sure QTD export includes:
- as_of_date (PT)
- rate_card_version
- attribution_model_version
- finance_ready flag

Laura Bennett: And a link to reconciliation view so we can answer questions quickly.

Talia Benson: Cool — I’ll capture this in the PM-483 checklist and follow up with owners. Thanks all.

title: #finance: 1734178920
preview:
Ava Chen: Hey finance — my corp card posted a charge `RIDE MULTIPLE 45.60 USD` on 2024-09-12 for airport -> hotel but there's no itemized invoice attached. I only have an in-app invoice screenshot and I tipped the driver $7 cash. Attaching screenshot now: [receipt_20240912.jpg]

expenses-bot: Policy note: tips <=20% allowed with manager approval. Charge flagged for missing itemized receipt and ambiguous merchant descriptor. Please upload invoice/receipt to Ramp or reply with details.

Marcus Li: Thanks @Ava — can you upload the ride app invoice or a card statement + a photo of any in-car receipt? Also confirm currency — card shows 45.60 USD, was this actually USD or local currency?

Ava Chen: Uploaded the in-app invoice PDF (Booking ID: `9F3-LK2`). Fare was charged in USD on the corp card. Tip was $7 cash to the driver, not on the card. [ride_invoice_9F3LK2.pdf]

Marcus Li: Got it. Since the tip was cash, finance can reimburse up to 20% with manager sign-off. @Samira can you approve the $7 cash gratuity and confirm business purpose?

Samira Patel: @Ava approved — client kickoff in SF, business-related. Approving $7 cash tip exception. :white_check_mark:

Jorge Ramos: For bookkeeping, please tag this as `Travel: Ground Transport` and set vendor to "Taxi / ride app (merchant descriptor ambiguous)". If this keeps happening we can try to pull better descriptors from the vendor.

Marcus Li: No vendor outreach needed for this ticket. Ava — please attach the PDF to the Ramp expense, set expense type to `cash_tip`, and add the booking ID in the notes. I will clear the missing-receipt flag once it's attached.

Ava Chen: Done — added the PDF to the Ramp expense and updated notes with booking ID. Thanks!

expenses-bot: Expense queued to AP for reimbursement. Estimated reimbursement: $7.00 USD (cash tip). Reference: EXP-7842.
